note: autocomplete_deluxe ships a single field widget (plugin id `autocomplete_deluxe`) for entity_reference fields, rendering a jQuery UI chip/tag box. There is no global settings form — configuration lives on each field's entity_form_display component (`field.widget.settings.autocomplete_deluxe`). Recipe cases are response-graded. Medium cases install a known field whose form display uses the widget and require live introspection. Hard cases build live config: switch a field's form-display widget to autocomplete_deluxe with a specific setting, verified against real entity_form_display state.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site I want to replace core's plain taxonomy autocomplete on a Tags field with a nicer jQuery UI tag-box (chips). Which contrib module provides this, what is the exact field-widget plugin id I select on Manage form display, and which field type(s) can it be applied to?
evals.expected_output: The Autocomplete Deluxe module. Its widget plugin id is `autocomplete_deluxe` (label "Autocomplete Deluxe"), and it applies to entity_reference fields (e.g. taxonomy term reference / Tags fields). It declares multiple_values = TRUE so one box holds all values.

evals.prompt: For the Drupal autocomplete_deluxe field widget, which per-field settings control (a) whether matching is prefix or substring, (b) how many suggestions are shown, (c) the minimum characters before the list opens, and (d) whether editors may create brand-new referenced terms? Give the setting keys and their default values.
evals.expected_output: match_operator (default CONTAINS; STARTS_WITH for prefix), match_limit (default 10; 0 = no limit), min_length (default 0), and new_terms (default false, free tagging). Stored under the form-display component settings, schema field.widget.settings.autocomplete_deluxe.

evals.prompt: Where do I configure the autocomplete_deluxe widget in Drupal — is there a dedicated admin settings page for the module, and does it define any Drush commands or permissions of its own?
evals.expected_output: There is no global admin settings form (configure is null) and no Drush commands or module-specific permissions. You configure it per field on Structure > Content types > Manage form display, which stores the widget on the field's entity_form_display component. The autocomplete route uses the core 'access content' permission.

evals.prompt: A taxonomy-tags reference field has been added to the Article content type on this site and its edit-form widget was customized. Inspect the Article node form-display configuration and tell me: which field widget is configured for that field, and what are its match operator, minimum length, and new-terms settings?
evals.expected_output: The field node.article.field_ac_intro uses the `autocomplete_deluxe` widget on the default form display, with settings match_operator = STARTS_WITH, min_length = 3, and new_terms = true.

evals.prompt: This site has an Article field (field_ac_intro) whose node-form widget is Autocomplete Deluxe. Inspect the live configuration and tell me what underlying FIELD TYPE that field is and what entity type it references — i.e. what kind of field the autocomplete_deluxe widget is being used on here.
evals.expected_output: field_ac_intro is an entity_reference field targeting taxonomy_term (a taxonomy term reference), multi-value (cardinality -1); the autocomplete_deluxe widget only applies to entity_reference fields.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site, configure the Article content type's default node edit form so the taxonomy term reference field field_eval_ac uses the Autocomplete Deluxe widget (plugin id autocomplete_deluxe) instead of its current core widget, and enable free tagging so editors can add brand-new terms (the new_terms setting). Apply it live so the entity_form_display reflects the change.
evals.expected_output: The node.article.default entity_form_display component for field_eval_ac has type = autocomplete_deluxe with settings.new_terms = true.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site, set the Article node edit form's widget for the taxonomy term reference field field_eval_tags to the autocomplete_deluxe widget, and configure it to match by prefix (match_operator = STARTS_WITH) rather than substring. Save it to the live entity_form_display.
evals.expected_output: The node.article.default entity_form_display component for field_eval_tags has type = autocomplete_deluxe with settings.match_operator = STARTS_WITH.
